groups.title: First day at work
groups.desc:
Questions 1-6
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
• Name of supervisor:                                     $21752$
• Where to leave coat and bag:                       use $21753$ in staffroom
• See Tiffany in HR:                                         to give $21754$ number
                                                                        to collect $21755$
• Location of HR office:                                   on $21756$ floor
• Supervisor’s mobile number:                       $21757$

questions.analysis: 答案是“Kaeden”，因为在对话开头，Kaeden自我介绍时明确说了“I'm Kaeden, one of the supervisors.”，这句话直接回答了题干中“Name of supervisor”的问题。定位词是“supervisor”，信号词是“one of the supervisors”，通过这些词可以准确找到答案句。
题干中的定位词是“coat and bag”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“coat and rucksack”。答案句明确提到“Put your coat and rucksack in one of the lockers there”，说明外套和背包需要放在储物柜里。因此，正确答案是“locker”或“lockers”。
答案是“passport”，因为听力材料中明确提到HR需要记录护照号码。通过定位词“HR”、“Tiffany”和“number”，考生可以快速找到相关信息，并根据上下文确定正确答案。学生需要特别注意听力材料中的关键词，并结合上下文进行推测和判断。
答案是“uniform”，因为在听力原文中，Kaeden明确提到Tiffany会给Charlotte一套制服（uniform），这与题干中的“to collect”相对应。定位词“collect”在原文中通过信号词“give”得到了提示，帮助我们确定了答案。

题干问的是HR办公室的位置，定位词是“HR office”。在听力原文中，Kaeden提到“HR are a couple of floors above that, on the third floor.”，这里的“HR”就是我们需要关注的信号词。通过理解“a couple of floors above that, on the third floor”这句话，可以确定HR办公室在三楼，所以正确答案是“third”或“3rd”。
题干中的定位词是“Supervisor’s mobile number”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“phone number”。当听到“phone number”时，考生应立即注意接下来的内容，因为这很可能就是答案所在的位置。听力原文中，凯登明确给出了他的手机号码“0412 665 903”，这就是正确答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 7-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content: Wipe preparation area and clean the sink
Clean the serving area, including the weighing scales
Collect$21760$for the fish from the cold-room

questions.analysis: 答案是“yellow”，因为听力原文明确提到“put a yellow one on the package, next to the original price”。通过抓住定位词“Use”和信号词“put a yellow one”，考生可以准确找到答案。
在听力材料中，Kaeden提到“flat cardboard boxes”和“plastic boxes”，并说明这些塑料盒子很快就会用完，需要从储藏室补充更多。这与题干中的“Re-stock with【8】boxes if needed”相对应。因此，正确答案是“plastic”。
正确答案是“ice”。在听力原文中，明确提到“鱼是放在冰上的，但当冰开始融化时，你需要从冷室中取出更多的冰”。这与题目中“Collect【9】for the fish from the cold-room”完全对应，因此答案是“ice”。
正确答案是“gloves”，因为在听力原文中明确提到在处理冷藏室的物品时需要戴上保暖手套（thermal gloves）。题目中的定位词“Meat and fish counters”和“Must wear special”帮助我们快速定位到相关信息，而信号词“put on thermal gloves”则直接提示了答案。

transcript.en: PART 1
KAEDEN: Hello Charlotte. I'm Kaeden, one of the supervisors. Welcome to the team.
transcript.cn: 凯登：你好，夏洛特。我是凯登，这里的主管之一。欢迎加入我们的团队。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Hi Aiden.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：你好，艾登。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: It's Kaeden.
transcript.cn: 凯登：是凯登。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: I'm so sorry.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：真不好意思。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: Don't worry. People often get my name wrong; they never know how to spell it.
transcript.cn: 凯登：没关系。大家经常弄错我的名字。他们不知道怎么拼。

transcript.en: It's K-A-E-D-E-N, in case you ever need to write it.
transcript.cn: 我的名字是 K-A-E-D-E-N，如果你需要写的话。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: I'll try and remember.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：我会尽量记住的。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: So, there are a few practical things you need to sort out this morning.
transcript.cn: 凯登：今天早上你需要解决一些实际问题。

transcript.en: Then I'll show you what you're going to do today.
transcript.cn: 然后我会告诉你你今天要做什么。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: The email I received said to go to the front desk, to show my letter of appointment and pick up my badge.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：我收到的邮件上说要去前台，出示我的任命书并领取徽章。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: You'll need that for the staffroom and other areas of the supermarket where shoppers aren't allowed.
transcript.cn: 凯登：你需要这个才能进入员工休息室和超市其他不允许购物者进入的区域。

transcript.en: So, after you've finished at the front desk, I'll take you to the staffroom.
transcript.cn: 在前台办完手续后，我带你去员工休息室。

transcript.en: Put your coat and rucksack in one of the lockers there. Take whichever one is free.
transcript.cn: 把你的外套和背包放在那边的一个储物柜里，随便挑一个空的就行。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Will I have a key?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：我会有钥匙吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: Yes. Try not to lose it. At the end of the day, leave it in the door for the next person to use.
transcript.cn: 凯登：有的。别弄丢了。一天结束时，把它放在门里，给下一个人用。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Will do.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: You also need to go to the HR department to see Tiffany. She's really helpful.
transcript.cn: 凯登：你还需要去人力资源部找蒂芙尼。她会给你很多帮助。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: I was told to bring my passport with me. HR need to take a note of the number in it.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：有人让我带上护照。人力资源部需要记下里面的号码。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: That's right. Or you can show your ID card.
transcript.cn: 凯登：没错，或者你可以出示身份证。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: I don't have one of those.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：我没有。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: OK. Tiffany will give you a uniform.
transcript.cn: 凯登：好的，蒂芙尼会给你一套工作服。

transcript.en: They have lots in different sizes, so you just tell her what you need.
transcript.cn: 它们有很多不同的尺寸，所以你只要告诉她你需要什么尺码。

transcript.en: I won't come with you to HR - I've got to go and sort something else out. Problem with a bread slicer.
transcript.cn: 我就不跟你去人事部了，我得去处理点别的事。面包切片机有点问题。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Is the HR office near the staffroom?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：人力资源办公室在员工休息室附近吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: The staffroom's on the first floor, and HR are a couple of floors above that, on the third floor. There's a staircase outside the staffroom.
transcript.cn: 凯登：员工休息室在一楼，人力资源部在那上面几层，在三楼。员工休息室外面有个楼梯。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: OK.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: When you've finished with HR, come and find me in the bakery section of the shop.
transcript.cn: 凯登：等你和人事部谈完了 就来店里的面包房找我。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: I'm looking forward to getting started.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：我很期待开始工作。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: I'll just give you my phone number, in case you can't find me. Have you got your phone there?
transcript.cn: 凯登：我把我的电话号码给你，以防你找不到我。你的手机在那儿吗？

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Yes . . . OK, ready.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：是的，好了，准备好了。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: It's oh-four-one-two double-six-five nine-oh-three.
CHARLOTTE: OK, done.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的，搞定。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: So, Charlotte, your tasks today are in the bakery section, on the sushi counter, and on the meat and fish counters.
transcript.cn: 凯登：那么，夏洛特，你今天的任务是在面包房、寿司柜台、肉类和鱼类柜台工作。

transcript.en: The first job is to check sell-by dates on the bread and cakes.
transcript.cn: 第一项工作是检查面包和蛋糕的保质期。

transcript.en: If any of the dates are today's, put a new price label on the packaging.
transcript.cn: 如果有任何日期是今天的，就在包装上贴上新的价格标签。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: What if any of the labels are yesterday's dates, or older?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：如果标签上的日期是昨天或更早怎么办？

transcript.en: Do I throw those items away?
transcript.cn: 我要扔掉这些商品吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: Yes, but that shouldn't happen - we check the stock every day.
transcript.cn: 凯登：是的，但这种情况不会发生，我们每天都会检查库存。

transcript.en: When something needs a new price label, put a yellow one on the package, next to the original price.
transcript.cn: 如果有东西需要新的价格标签，就在包装上贴一个黄色标签，贴在原价旁边。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: OK.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: After that, you'll go to the sushi takeaway counter.
transcript.cn: 凯登：然后，你去寿司外卖柜台。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Will I be preparing boxes of food?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：我要准备盒装食物吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: For today, you'll just be helping the staff.
transcript.cn: 凯登：今天，你只需要帮助其他员工。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Yes, of course.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的，没问题。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: You'll see lots of flat cardboard boxes at one end of the counter.
transcript.cn: 凯登：你会在柜台的一侧看到很多平板纸箱。

transcript.en: Beneath those is where we keep the plastic boxes - we run out of those really quickly, so you should bring more from the storeroom.
transcript.cn: 下面是我们放塑料盒的地方——我们的塑料盒很快就会用完，所以你应该从储藏室多拿些来。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Is that my only task on the sushi counter?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：这是我在寿司柜台的唯一任务吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: No. You also need to clean the area where they prepare the dishes.
transcript.cn: 凯登：不，你还需要清洁准备餐具的区域。

transcript.en: There are cloths and bottles of spray by the sink. Oh, and please make sure you clean that too.
transcript.cn: 水槽旁有抹布和喷雾瓶。对了，请务必把那里也打扫干净。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Sure. That's important, isn't it?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：当然，这很重要，不是吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: Absolutely. But you mustn't wash up knives.
transcript.cn: 凯登：没错，但你不能清洗刀具。

transcript.en: You have to do some training before you're allowed to touch sharp objects.
transcript.cn: 在接触尖锐物体之前，你必须做一些培训。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: What should I do if there are any?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：如果有的话，我该怎么办？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: Ask someone to put them in the dishwasher.
transcript.cn: 凯登：叫人把他们放进洗碗机里。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: OK, thanks. I don't want to get anything wrong.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的，谢谢。我不想弄错任何东西。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: Don't worry. You'll be fine. And I'll be around to help.
transcript.cn: 凯登：别担心，你会做好的。我会在这里帮助你。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Right.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的。

transcript.en: KAEDEN: Finally, the meat and fish counters.
transcript.cn: 凯登：最后是肉类和鱼类柜台。

transcript.en: You need to clean the area where staff serve customers, including wiping the weighing scales.
transcript.cn: 你需要清洁员工服务顾客的区域，包括擦拭称重秤。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: OK. Anything else?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：好的。还有别的事情吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: The fish is laid on ice, but when that starts to melt, you'll need to get more from the cold-room.
transcript.cn: 凯登：鱼是放在冰上的，但当它开始融化，你需要从冷藏室再拿一些。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: I know the staff on the food counters wear a hat. Will that be the same for me?
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：我注意到食品柜台的工作人员都戴帽子。我也要戴帽子吗？

transcript.en: KAEDEN: You won't be serving customers directly, so no.
transcript.cn: 凯登：你不会直接为顾客服务，所以不用。

transcript.en: But make sure you put on thermal gloves when you take anything out of the cold-room.
transcript.cn: 但从冷藏室拿东西时一定要戴上保暖手套。

transcript.en: The temperature's low enough in there to get frostbite from touching things.
transcript.cn: 里面的温度很低，碰到东西会冻伤的。

transcript.en: CHARLOTTE: Understood.
transcript.cn: 夏洛特：明白。

groups.title: Which TWO problems with some training programmes for new runners does Liz mention?
groups.desc:
Questions 11-12
Choose TWO letters, A–E
Write your answers in boxes 11-12 on your answer sheet.

list.title: A
list.content: There is a risk of serious injury.

list.title: B
list.content: They are unsuitable for certain age groups.

list.title: C
list.content: They are unsuitable for people with health issues.

list.title: D
list.content: It is difficult to stay motivated.

list.title: E
list.content: There is a lack of individual support.

collect_option.title: Which TWO problems with some training programmes for new runners does Liz mention?

questions.analysis: 这道题目要求考生找出Liz提到的关于新跑者训练计划的两个问题。通过听力原文，我们可以确定两个问题：1）这些训练计划不适用于有健康问题的人（如心脏病或哮喘）；2）缺乏个性化支持（没有人能回答具体问题）。因此，正确答案是C和E。
这道题目要求考生找出Liz提到的关于新跑者训练计划的两个问题。通过听力原文，我们可以确定两个问题：1）这些训练计划不适用于有健康问题的人（如心脏病或哮喘）；2）缺乏个性化支持（没有人能回答具体问题）。因此，正确答案是C和E。

groups.title: Which TWO tips does Liz recommend for new runners?
groups.desc:
Questions 13-14
Choose TWO letters, A–E
Write your answers in boxes 13-14 on your answer sheet.

list.title: C
list.content: going on runs with a friend

collect_option.title: Which TWO tips does Liz recommend for new runners?

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 A 和 D。Liz 推荐新跑者每周跑两次，并且在跑步时听音乐。她提到每周两次的训练足够开始，并且听音乐可以帮助新跑者分心并进入节奏。
本题的正确答案是 A 和 D。Liz 推荐新跑者每周跑两次，并且在跑步时听音乐。她提到每周两次的训练足够开始，并且听音乐可以帮助新跑者分心并进入节奏。

groups.title: Reasons
groups.desc:
Questions 15-18
What reason prevented each of the following members of the Compton Park Runners Club from joining until recently?
Write the correct letter, A, B, or C next to Questions 15–18.

questions.title: Ceri
questions.analysis: 根据原文，Ceri 迟迟没有加入俱乐部的原因是她缺乏信心，担心自己会被落下，成为最慢的跑者。这表明她缺乏信心，因此正确答案是 A. a lack of confidence。

questions.title: James
questions.analysis: James 之前没有加入跑步俱乐部的主要原因是他讨厌跑步，这在原文中通过“hated the idea of running”明确表达。因此，选项 B “a dislike of running” 是正确答案。

questions.title: Leo
questions.analysis: Leo因为担心无法将训练安排进繁忙的日程而迟迟没有加入跑步俱乐部。因此，正确答案是C. a lack of time。学生在做这道题时，应特别注意定位词和信号词的使用，抓住关键信息，从而准确作答。

questions.title: Mark
questions.analysis: Mark的主要问题是缺乏信心，因为他花了几个月的时间才鼓起勇气联系俱乐部。这与选项A最为符合。

groups.desc:
Questions 19 and 20
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What does Liz say about running her first marathon?

options.title: A
options.content: It had always been her ambition.

options.title: B
options.content: Her husband persuaded her to do it.

options.title: C
options.content: She nearly gave up before the end.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为听力原文明确提到她在跑马拉松的过程中“felt like giving up halfway through”，这与选项C“她差点在结束前放弃”相符。学生在听力过程中应特别注意定位词“first marathon”以及信号词“giving up”，以便准确捕捉到答案。

questions.title: Liz says new runners should sign up for a race

options.title: A
options.content: every six months.

options.title: B
options.content: within a few weeks of taking up running.

options.title: C
options.content: after completing several practice runs.

questions.analysis: Liz建议新跑者在开始跑步几周后报名参加比赛，而不是每六个月或完成几次练习后。她提到“after you've been training for a few weeks, it's worth putting your name down for a 5K”，这明确指出了答案是B。

transcript.en: PART 2
My name's Liz Fuller and I'm a running coach with Compton Park Runners Club.
transcript.cn: 我叫丽兹·富勒，是康普顿公园跑步俱乐部的一名跑步教练。

transcript.en: Welcome to my podcast. If you're thinking about taking up running - I'm here to help.
transcript.cn: 欢迎收听我的播客。如果你想开始跑步，我可以为您提供帮助。

transcript.en: There are many training programmes available online which aim to help people build up to running 5 kilometres.
transcript.cn: 网上有很多训练计划，旨在帮助人们提高跑5公里的能力。

transcript.en: Some of them are great and thousands of people of all ages are taking part in 5-kilometre races across the country as a result.
transcript.cn: 其中有些训练计划非常好，因此全国各地有成千上万不同年龄的人参加了5公里赛跑。

transcript.en: People like them because they're easy to follow and don't push them too hard.
transcript.cn: 人们之所以喜欢这些训练方案，是因为它们简单易学，不会对他们造成太大的压力。

transcript.en: However, they don't work for everyone - especially if you suffer from something like a heart condition or asthma, because they're aimed at people with average fitness and running ability.
transcript.cn: 但是，它们并不适合所有人，尤其是患有心脏病或哮喘的人，因为它们针对的是体能和跑步能力正常的人。

transcript.en: Another thing is that everyone is different - and if you have any specific questions related to your needs, there's no one to provide any answers.
transcript.cn: 另外，每个人的情况都不一样，如果您有任何与自身需求相关的具体问题，也没有人可以提供答案。

transcript.en: I have a couple of simple tips I always give to new runners.
transcript.cn: 我经常给新跑步者一些简单的建议。

transcript.en: I expect you've been told to run very slowly until your fitness increases - well, I find that can prevent progress.
transcript.cn: 我猜有人告诉过你要跑得很慢，直到你的体能增强为止--但我发现这会阻碍你取得进步。

transcript.en: You should run at a speed that feels comfortable, but time yourself and try to run a bit faster each time.
transcript.cn: 你应该以感觉舒适的速度跑步，但要给自己计时，每次都要试着跑快一点。

transcript.en: Listening to music can be very helpful - it takes your mind off things and helps your body get into a rhythm.
transcript.cn: 听音乐会很有帮助--它能让你忘却烦恼，帮助你的身体进入节奏。

transcript.en: I'd say that is better than running with a friend - especially as most people are competitive and that's not what you want when you're just starting.
transcript.cn: 我认为这比和朋友一起跑步更好--尤其是大多数人都有好胜心，而这并不是你刚开始跑步时想要的。

transcript.en: I don't think the time of day is especially important - some people are better in the evening, while others are morning people - but you need to be consistent, so aim to train regularly - twice a week is enough to begin with.
transcript.cn: 我认为一天中跑步的时间并不特别重要--有些人在晚上跑得更好，而有些人则在早上--但你需要坚持不懈，所以要以定期训练为目标--开始时一周两次就足够了。

transcript.en: New members often say to me that they've been put off running either because they lack confidence, or they don't have time, or they think they dislike running.
transcript.cn: 新会员经常对我说，他们之所以不愿意跑步，要么是因为缺乏自信，要么是因为没有时间，要么是因为他们认为自己不喜欢跑步。

transcript.en: Ceri, for example, joined the club two years ago at the age of 40.
transcript.cn: 例如，两年前，40 岁的切里加入了俱乐部。

transcript.en: She'd always enjoyed running at school but wasn't sure if she'd be able to do it.
transcript.cn: 她在学校一直很喜欢跑步，但不确定自己能否坚持下去。

transcript.en: She was worried about being left behind and being the slowest runner.
transcript.cn: 她担心自己会落在后面，成为跑得最慢的人。

transcript.en: But she says she was made to feel so welcome she soon forgot all about that.
transcript.cn: 但她说，她觉得自己很受欢迎，以至于很快就忘记了这一切。

transcript.en: James had always hated the idea of running but a friend encouraged him to come along for a taster session and he hasn't looked back.
transcript.cn: 詹姆斯一直不喜欢跑步，但在一位朋友的鼓励下，他参加了跑步体验课，从此一发不可收。

transcript.en: He never misses a training session despite having a really demanding job.
transcript.cn: 尽管工作非常繁重，但他从未缺席过训练课。

transcript.en: Leo was worried about having to commit himself to training sessions every week and wasn't sure he'd be able to fit training into his busy schedule.
transcript.cn: 利奥担心自己每周都必须参加训练课程，他不确定自己是否能在繁忙的日程中安排好训练。

transcript.en: But after experiencing a lot of stress at work he came along to us and gave it a go.
transcript.cn: 不过，在经历了巨大的工作压力之后，他来到我们这里，试着接受了训练。

transcript.en: Now he says he feels much more relaxed and he looks forward to his weekly run.
transcript.cn: 现在他说他感觉轻松多了，而且他期待着每周的跑步。

transcript.en: Mark is quite typical of our new members.
transcript.cn: 马克是我们新会员中的典型代表。

transcript.en: He's never considered himself to be a sporty person and it was only when he retired that he decided to take up the challenge of trying to run 5 kilometres.
transcript.cn: 他从来不认为自己是一个爱好运动的人，直到退休后，他才决定接受尝试跑5公里的挑战。

transcript.en: It took him months to find the courage to contact us but felt reassured immediately as there were other people his age who were only just taking up running for the first time.
transcript.cn: 他花了几个月的时间才鼓起勇气联系我们，但他马上就放心了，因为还有其他同龄人才刚刚开始尝试跑步。

transcript.en: My own journey hasn't been easy.
transcript.cn: 我自己的跑步之路也并不轻松。

transcript.en: I did my first marathon when I was 37, after having had two kids.
transcript.cn: 我在 37 岁时第一次参加马拉松比赛，当时我已经有了两个孩子。

transcript.en: My husband had been running marathons for years, but I never dreamed I'd be doing one with him.
transcript.cn: 我的丈夫已经跑了多年马拉松，但我从未想过我会和他一起跑马拉松。

transcript.en: I managed to complete it in four hours, but I felt like giving up halfway through - it was only the support of the spectators that kept me going.
transcript.cn: 我尝试在四小时内完成了比赛，但中途我感觉要放弃了——是观众的支持让我坚持了下来。

transcript.en: I do think signing up for a race of whatever length is motivating - whether it's 5K or 25K - because it's good to have something to work towards and it gives you a sense of achievement.
transcript.cn: 我确实认为，报名参加任何长度的比赛都是一种动力——不管是 5 公里还是 25 公里——因为有目标去努力是件好事，而且能给你带来成就感。

transcript.en: I did my first 10K after only six months, which was certainly very challenging and not something I'd necessarily recommend.
transcript.cn: 我只用了 6 个月就跑完了我的第一个 10 公里赛程，这当然非常具有挑战性，我也不一定会推荐这么做。

transcript.en: But after you've been training for a few weeks, it's worth putting your name down for a 5K - some people find they only need a few practice runs before taking part in a race, but I'd give yourself a couple of months at least.
transcript.cn: 但在你训练了几周后，不妨报名参加 5 公里赛跑——有些人发现他们在参加比赛前只需要进行几次练习，但我建议你至少给自己几个月的时间。

transcript.en: Well, I hope that's given . . .
transcript.cn: 好吧，我希望你能明白……

groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Kieran thinks the packing advice given by Jane’s grandfather is

options.title: A
options.content: common sense.

options.title: B
options.content: hard to follow.

options.title: C
options.content: over-protective.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A. common sense。Kieran在听到Jane的祖父关于如何打包书籍的建议后，表示“that's obvious”，这表明他认为这些建议是常识性的。通过定位词和信号词的分析，可以帮助学生更准确地找到答案所在的句子，并理解其含义。

questions.title: How does Jane feel about the books her grandfather has given her

options.title: A
options.content: They are not worth keeping.

options.title: B
options.content: They should go to a collector.

options.title: C
options.content: They have sentimental value for her.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C，因为Jane明确表示这些书让她想起祖父，并且她会后悔扔掉它们，这表明这些书对她有情感价值。通过定位词和信号词的分析，学生可以更准确地找到答案所在的句子，并理解Jane对这些书的情感态度。

questions.title: Jane and Kieran agree that hardback books should be

options.title: A
options.content: put out on display.

options.title: B
options.content: given as gifts to visitors.

options.title: C
options.content: more attractively designed.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A“put out on display”，因为对话中明确提到人们不再像以前那样把书放在书架上展示，而Kieran认为这是很遗憾的事情，并且他会被桌子上的彩色书籍吸引。通过定位词“hardback books”和信号词“keep them out on shelves”以及“a colourful book on a table”，可以准确找到答案。

questions.title: While talking about taking a book from a shelf, Jane

options.title: A
options.content: describes the mistakes other people make doing it.

options.title: B
options.content: reflects on a significant childhood experience.

options.title: C
options.content: explains why some books are easier to remove than others.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为Jane在对话中回忆了她小时候从书架上取书的经历，这是一段重要的童年记忆。通过抓住定位词和信号词，学生可以更准确地定位到相关信息，从而选择正确答案。

questions.title: What do Jane and Kieran suggest about new books?

options.title: A
options.content: Their parents liked buying them as presents.

options.title: B
options.content: They would like to buy more of them.

options.title: C
options.content: Not everyone can afford them.

questions.analysis: 在听力材料中，Jane提到“New books aren't cheap, though, are they?”，这直接表明新书的价格不便宜。Kieran回应说“we're lucky we can buy them”，暗示并不是所有人都能负担得起新书。因此，选项C“Not everyone can afford them”是正确答案。

groups.title: Location of books
groups.desc:
Questions 26-30
Where does Jane’s grandfather keep each of the following types of books in his shop?
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–G, next to Questions 26–30.

list.title: C
list.content: at the back of the shop

questions.title: rare books
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D. on a high shelf。听力原文中提到这些稀有书籍被放在需要梯子才能拿到的高处，这与选项D完全吻合。学生在做题时应特别注意定位词和信号词的使用，以便快速准确地找到答案。

questions.title: children’s books
questions.analysis: 正确答案是 F. in a specially designed space。根据原文，Jane 提到她的祖父在一楼有一个专门的区域，里面有垫子，父母可以带着他们的幼儿进去，在那里花些时间读书。这表明儿童书籍被放置在一个专门设计的空间里。

questions.title: unwanted books
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，Jane的祖父将“不需要的书籍”放在“前门旁边”，供顾客拿取。因此，正确答案是A. near the entrance。学生在听力过程中需要特别注意“unwanted books”这个定位词，并在听到后关注其放置位置的描述。

questions.title: requested books
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C. at the back of the shop。题干中的定位词是“requested books”，在听力原文中出现了“requested”这个信号词。答案句中提到“they all go at the far end”，即这些书籍都放在书店的后端，这与选项C相符。

questions.title: coursebooks
questions.analysis: 正确答案是G. within the café。听力原文明确提到课程书籍现在被放在咖啡店的低书架上，这与选项G完全匹配。学生在听力过程中需要特别注意“coursebooks”和“coffee shop”这两个关键词，并结合上下文理解其具体位置。

transcript.en: PART 3
KIERAN: So Jane - you'll be off to Denmark soon to do your work placement.
transcript.cn: 基兰：简，你很快就要去丹麦实习了。

transcript.en: JANE: Yes, I'm really looking forward to it and I've just started packing up all my books to put in storage.
transcript.cn: 简：是的，我真的很期待，我刚开始收拾我所有的书，准备放进仓库。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Well, I hope they don't get spoilt.
transcript.cn: 基兰：希望它们不会被弄坏。

transcript.en: JANE: It's OK - my grandfather works in a bookshop and he told me how to pack them.
transcript.cn: 简：没事的，我爷爷在书店工作，他告诉我怎么打包。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Oh, that's helpful.
transcript.cn: 基兰：这很有用。

transcript.en: JANE: He says you have to support the spine otherwise the paper can come away from the cover.
transcript.cn: 简：他说必须支撑住书脊，否则纸张会从封面上脱落。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Yeah - that's obvious.
transcript.cn: 基兰：是的，很显然。

transcript.en: JANE: He also told me to pack them flat in the box not on their side - again because they can bend and if you leave them like that for, say, a year, it's quite hard to get them back to their normal shape.
transcript.cn: 简：他还告诉我要把书平放在盒子里，不要侧放，因为书会弯曲，如果你把书这样放一年，就很难让它们恢复正常形状了。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Well, it's pretty clear that ruins them, but a lot of people just can't be bothered to protect their books.
transcript.cn: 基兰：很明显这会毁了他们，但很多人就是懒得保护他们的书。

transcript.en: JANE: He always says it's such a shame that publishers don't use better-quality paper.
transcript.cn: 简: 爷爷总是说，出版商不使用质量更好的纸张真是太可惜了。

transcript.en: KIERAN: It's the acid in the paper that causes the problem, isn't it?
transcript.cn: 基兰：纸张里的酸性物质才是问题所在，不是吗？

transcript.en: JANE: Yeah - that's why old books go yellow. You know some of the books my grandfather's given me are like that already.
transcript.cn: 简：是啊，这就是为什么旧书会变黄。你知道我爷爷给我的一些书就已经是这样了。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Oh . . .
transcript.cn: 基兰：天啊……

transcript.en: JANE: I should dump them really if they're going to deteriorate further, but I'd feel bad.
transcript.cn: 简：如果这些书继续变黄的话，我真应该把它们扔了，但是又觉得很可惜。

transcript.en: They'll always remind me of him. He's quite a collector, you know.
transcript.cn: 这些书总是让我想起我爷爷，他是个收藏家。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Well, if they're important to you . . .
transcript.cn: 基兰：是的，如果它们对你很重要的话. . .

transcript.en: JANE: Yeah - I'd regret just throwing them away.
transcript.cn: 简：是啊，要是把它们扔掉我会后悔的。

transcript.en: KIERAN: You know, maybe it's because I was taught to treasure books . . . but I hate seeing students force open the pages - of paperbacks. They press so hard they end up breaking the spine.
transcript.cn: 基兰：也许是因为我从小就被教育要爱惜书本吧，但我最讨厌看到学生们用力撕开平装书的书页，他们用力过猛，最后把书脊都压断了。

transcript.en: JANE: I know, but unfortunately, paperbacks aren't designed to last a long time and people know that. Hardbacks aren't quite as weak.
transcript.cn: 简：我知道，但不幸的是，平装书的设计并不耐用，人们也知道这一点。精装书就没那么脆弱了。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Yeah, they're different, I suppose. But I still don't think people value hardbacks like they used to.
transcript.cn: 基兰：是的，我想它们是不一样的。但是我仍然认为人们不像以前那样看重精装书。

transcript.en: JANE: Well, they aren't decorative, are they, like other objects. Plus, nowadays, people don't keep them out on shelves as much as they used to.
transcript.cn: 简：精装书不像其他物品那样具有装饰性，不是吗？另外，现在人们不再像以前那样把精装书放在书架上了。

transcript.en: KIERAN: That's such a pity. When I visit someone - if they have, say, a colourful book on a table, it's the first thing I'm drawn to.
transcript.cn: 基兰：真遗憾。我去拜访别人时，如果他们的桌子上摆着一本色彩缤纷的书，我首先就会被它吸引。

transcript.en: JANE: I agree - and book covers can be a work of art in themselves. Some are really eye-catching.
transcript.cn: 简：我同意，书的封面本身就是一件艺术品。有些封面真的很引人注目。

transcript.en: KIERAN: I've always been taught to handle books carefully. If you watch someone take a book off a shelf, well, they usually do it wrong.
transcript.cn: 基兰：我一直被教导要小心处理书籍。如果你看到有人从书架上取下一本书，那么，通常他们取书的方式是错的。

transcript.en: JANE: Ah, my grandfather says, you should put your hand right over the top of the book . . . or if you can't do that, pull the other books on the shelf aside so that you can hold the whole cover.
transcript.cn: 简：我爷爷说，应该把手放在书的正上方，或者如果做不到，把书架上的其他书拉到一边，这样就可以把整个封面都拿起来。

transcript.en: KIERAN: When did you learn all this?
transcript.cn: 基兰：你什么时候学会这些的？

transcript.en: JANE: He watched me pull a heavy book off the shelf when I was small, and it fell on the floor and broke apart.
transcript.cn: 简：小时候，他看着我从书架上取下一本很重的书，书掉在地上摔散架了。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Oh dear!
transcript.cn: 基兰：天哪！

transcript.en: JANE: I can still remember it!
transcript.cn: 简：我一直记到现在！

transcript.en: KIERAN: You know what I really like?
transcript.cn: 基兰：你知道我最喜欢什么吗？

transcript.en: JANE: What?
transcript.cn: 简：是什么？

transcript.en: KIERAN: The smell of new books.
transcript.cn: 基兰：新书的味道。

transcript.en: JANE: Me too.
transcript.cn: 简：我也是。

transcript.en: KIERAN: My parents used to laugh at me when I was a kid because I loved putting books up to my nose. Almost as much as reading them!
transcript.cn: 基兰：小时候，我父母常常嘲笑我，因为我喜欢把书放在鼻子上。几乎和我喜欢读书一样不相上下！

transcript.en: JANE: New books aren't cheap, though, are they?
transcript.cn: 简: 不过新书并不便宜，不是吗？

transcript.en: KIERAN: I guess we're lucky we can buy them.
transcript.cn: 基兰：我想我们能买到它们已经很幸运了。

transcript.en: JANE: My grandfather stocks second-hand books as well as new ones and they don't smell quite as good.
transcript.cn: 简: 我爷爷存放二手书和新书，它们闻起来没有那么好。

transcript.en: KIERAN: I'd love to have a bookshop like your grandfather. What's it like?
transcript.cn: 基兰：我很想像你爷爷那样开一家书店。它是什么样的？

transcript.en: JANE: Well, it's quite big - it's got two floors and an attic, and he stocks all kinds of books really.
transcript.cn: 简：嗯，挺大的，有两层楼，还有阁楼，什么书都有。

transcript.en: KIERAN: I guess he treasures things like first editions and other rare books.
transcript.cn: 基兰：我猜他珍藏的有初版书和其他稀有书籍。

transcript.en: JANE: Yeah - you might think he'd keep those in the attic or somewhere.
transcript.cn: 简：是啊，你可能以为他会把那些书放在阁楼或什么地方。

transcript.en: KIERAN: . . . so they'd be hidden?
transcript.cn: 基兰：所以这些书会被藏起来吗？

transcript.en: JANE: Yeah. But he likes people to know that he has them.
transcript.cn: 简: 是的。但是他又喜欢让人们知道他拥有他们。

transcript.en: So, he puts them out in the shop but makes sure you need a ladder to get them.
transcript.cn: 所以他把这些书就放在店里，但确保你需要梯子才能拿到它们

transcript.en: KIERAN: Right. That would prevent any thefts!
transcript.cn: 基兰：没错，这样就不会被偷了。

transcript.en: JANE: Uhuh.
transcript.cn: 简：嗯。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Does he stock books for children?
transcript.cn: 基兰：他有儿童读物吗？

transcript.en: JANE: He does. He particularly likes to encourage kids to read; he always says that he used to sit under the stairs as a child with a pile of books and read them all.
transcript.cn: 简：是的。他特别喜欢鼓励孩子们读书，他总是说他小时候常常坐在楼梯下面，手里拿着一堆书，把它们全部读完。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Is that where he keeps them, then?
transcript.cn: 基兰：那他就把书放在那里吗？

transcript.en: JANE: Not exactly - he's got a dedicated area on the ground floor with cushions so that parents can enter with their toddlers, go there and spend some time reading to them.
transcript.cn: 简：不完全是--他在一楼有一个专门的区域，铺上软垫，这样家长们就可以带着他们的孩子进去，到那里花一些时间给他们读书。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Oh cool.
transcript.cn: 基兰：太酷了。

transcript.en: JANE: And then there's a place for pushchairs by the front door. And a café if anyone needs refreshments.
transcript.cn: 简: 前门还有个地方可以放手推车。如果有人需要点心，还可以去咖啡馆。

transcript.en: KIERAN: That's good to know.
transcript.cn: 基兰：听起来很不错。

transcript.en: JANE: As I said, it's a big shop and there's a storage area out the back as well.
transcript.cn: 简：正如我所说，这是一家大店铺，后面还有一个储藏室。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Oh, what does he keep there? Books he wants to throw away?
transcript.cn: 基兰：哦，他在哪里都放了什么？ 他想扔掉的书？

transcript.en: JANE: He hardly ever throws anything away - he just leaves unwanted books by the front door for customers to take.
transcript.cn: 简：他几乎不扔任何东西--他只是把不想要的书放在前门，让顾客拿走。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Well, that's very nice.
transcript.cn: 基兰：嗯，真不错。

transcript.en: JANE: Yeah - and books people or institutions have requested, they all go at the far end.
transcript.cn: 简：是的，人们或机构要求的书籍都放在最里面。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Oh.
transcript.cn: 基兰：哦。

transcript.en: JANE: He thinks it's best to keep these out of the main shopping area as they're boxed and new.
transcript.cn: 简：他认为这些书最好不要放在主要购物区，因为它们都是装箱的新书。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Did you get your coursebooks from him?
transcript.cn: 基兰：你的课本是从他那里拿的吗？

transcript.en: JANE: Naturally. He stocks books for a lot of the colleges.
transcript.cn: 简：那是自然。他为很多大学储备书籍。

transcript.en: He used to keep these books on the first floor, but now there's a new university in my hometown, he's moved them downstairs to attract the students.
transcript.cn: 他以前把这些书放在一楼，但现在我家乡新开了一所大学，他就把书搬到楼下，以吸引学生。

transcript.en: They're actually part of the coffee shop, on low shelves all around it.
transcript.cn: 它们实际上是咖啡店的一部分，在咖啡店周围低矮的架子上。

transcript.en: KIERAN: Pretty central then. You'll have to take me there some time!
transcript.cn: 基兰：那就是中心了，你得找个时间带我去那里！

groups.title: Tree planting
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Reforestation projects should: • include a range of tree species • not include invasive species because of possible $21782$ with native species • aim to capture carbon, protect the environment and provide sustainable sources of $21783$ for local people • use tree seeds with a high genetic diversity to increase resistance to $21784$ and climate change • plant trees on previously forested land which is in a bad condition, not select land which is being used for $21785$ Large-scale reforestation projects • Base planning decisions on information from accurate $21786$ • Drones are useful for identifying areas in Brazil which are endangered by keeping $21787$ and illegal logging. Lampang Province, Northern Thailand • A forest was restored in an area damaged by mining. • A variety of native fig trees were planted, which are important for - supporting many wildlife species - increasing the $21788$ of recovery by attracting animals and birds, e.g., $21789$ were soon attracted to the area. Involving local communities • Destruction of mangrove forests in Madagascar made it difficult for people to make a living from $21790$ • The mangrove reforestation project: - provided employment for local people - restored a healthy ecosystem - protects against the higher risk of $21791$

questions.analysis: 答案是“competition”，因为听力材料中明确提到“invasive species are often in competition with native species”。考生需要通过定位词“invasive species”快速找到相关句子，并理解句子中的关键单词“competition”。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键词和猜测答案来提高正确率。

题干中的定位词“capture carbon”, “protect the environment”, “sustainable sources”, “local people”在听力原文中都有对应的信号词。特别是“sustainable sources”后面的内容直接指向了答案“food”。原文提到“improve the availability of food for the local community”，这与题干中的“provide sustainable sources of”相对应，因此答案是“food”。

正确答案是“disease”，因为听力原文中明确提到“Using seeds with low genetic diversity generally lowers the resilience of restored forests, which can make them vulnerable to disease and unable to adapt to climate change.” 这句话解释了遗传多样性低的种子会使森林容易受到疾病的侵害，并且无法适应气候变化。因此，题干中提到的“use tree seeds with a high genetic diversity to increase resistance to ... and climate change”中的空格应填入“disease”，以表示提高对疾病和气候变化的抵抗力。
本题的正确答案是“agriculture”，因为听力原文明确提到“Reforesting areas which are currently exploited for agriculture should be avoided”，这与题干中的“not select land”相对应。通过定位词和信号词的分析，学生可以更准确地找到答案所在的句子，并从中提取正确的单词。

这道题的正确答案是“maps”，因为听力原文中明确提到“Having detailed and up-to-date maps identifying high-priority areas for intervention is essential.”，这句话中的“maps”直接对应题干中的“accurate information”。通过定位词“planning decisions”和“accurate information”，以及信号词“detailed and up-to-date maps”，可以准确找到答案。
答案是“cattle”，因为题干中提到无人机在巴西用于识别受到威胁的区域，具体是由于“illegal logging”（非法砍伐）和另一种活动。听力原文中提到“rearing cattle”（养牛）和“illegal logging”是导致亚马逊部分地区被破坏的活动。因此，正确答案是“cattle”。

答案是“speed”，因为听力原文中明确提到“accelerate the speed of the recovery process by attracting animals and birds”，这与题干中的“increasing the 【    】 of recovery by attracting animals and birds”完全对应。通过定位词“recovery”和“attracting animals and birds”，可以迅速找到相关句子，并确定答案为“speed”。
题干中的定位词是“attracted”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“started visiting”。听力原文提到“monkeys started visiting to eat the fig fruits”，说明猴子被吸引到该区域。因此，正确答案是“monkeys”。

题干问的是红树林的破坏使得人们难以从事什么谋生方式。听力原文中提到“Destruction of the mangrove forests... badly affected the fishing industry, which was a major source of employment for local people living in coastal areas.”，这表明渔业是当地人主要的谋生方式，因此答案是“fishing”。

题干中的定位词是“higher risk”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“increased threat”。答案句中提到“mangroves also act as a defence against the increased threat of flooding caused by climate change”，说明红树林可以防御由气候变化引起的洪水威胁。因此，正确答案是“flooding”。

transcript.en: PART 4
Tree planting now dominates political and popular agendas and is often presented as an easy answer to the climate crisis, as well as a way for business corporations to offset their carbon emissions.
transcript.cn: 植树造林现在已成为政治和大众议程的主要内容，并经常被视为解决气候危机的简便办法，也是商业公司抵消碳排放的一种方式。

transcript.en: But unfortunately, tree planting isn't as straightforward as some people think.
transcript.cn: 但遗憾的是，植树并不像有些人想象的那样简单。

transcript.en: When the wrong trees are planted in the wrong place, it can do considerably more damage than good, failing to help either people or the environment.
transcript.cn: 如果在错误的地方种植了错误的树木，可能会弊大于利，既无益于人类，也无益于环境。

transcript.en: Reforestation projects are currently being undertaken on a huge scale in many countries and it's crucial that the right trees are selected.
transcript.cn: 目前，许多国家正在开展大规模的植树造林项目，选择正确的树木至关重要。

transcript.en: A mix of species should always be planted, typical of the local natural forest ecosystem and including rare and endangered species in order to create a rich ecosystem.
transcript.cn: 为了创造一个丰富的生态系统，应该种植当地自然生态系统的典型混合树种，包括稀有和濒危树种。

transcript.en: It's important to avoid non-native species that could become invasive.
transcript.cn: 避免外来物种入侵是很重要的。

transcript.en: Invasive species are a significant contributor to the current global biodiversity crisis and are often in competition with native species and may threaten their long-term survival.
transcript.cn: 入侵物种是造成当前全球生物多样性危机的一个重要因素，它们经常与本地物种竞争，可能威胁到本地物种的长期生存。

transcript.en: Restoring biodiversity that will maximise carbon capture is key when reforesting an area, but ideally any reforestation project should have several goals.
transcript.cn: 在重新造林时，恢复生物多样性以最大限度地捕获碳是关键，但理想情况下，任何重新造林项目都应该有几个目标。

transcript.en: These could include selecting trees that can contribute to wildlife conservation, improve the availability of food for the local community and maintain the stability of soil systems.
transcript.cn: 这些目标可以包括选择有助于野生动物保护的树木，改善当地社区的食物供应，以及保持土壤系统的稳定。

transcript.en: Meeting as many of these goals as possible, whilst doing no harm to local communities, native ecosystems and vulnerable species, is the sign of a highly successful tree-planting scheme.
transcript.cn: 尽可能多地实现这些目标，同时不损害当地社区、本地生态系统和脆弱物种，是植树计划取得巨大成功的标志。

transcript.en: To ensure the survival and resilience of a planted forest, it's vital to use tree seeds with appropriate levels of genetic diversity: the amount of genetic variation found within a species essential for their survival.
transcript.cn: 为确保植树造林的存活率和恢复力，使用具有适当水平的遗传多样性的树种至关重要：即在一个物种中发现的对其生存至关重要的遗传变异量。

transcript.en: Using seeds with low genetic diversity generally lowers the resilience of restored forests, which can make them vulnerable to disease and unable to adapt to climate change.
transcript.cn: 使用遗传多样性较低的种子通常会降低恢复后森林的复原力，使其易受疾病侵袭，无法适应气候变化。

transcript.en: Choosing the right location for reforestation projects is as important as choosing the right trees.
transcript.cn: 选择合适的造林地点与选择合适的树木同样重要。

transcript.en: Ultimately, the best area for planting trees would be in formerly forested areas that are in poor condition.
transcript.cn: 归根结底，植树造林的最佳地点是条件恶劣的原林区。

transcript.en: It's better to avoid non-forested landscapes such as natural grasslands, savannas or wetlands as these ecosystems already contribute greatly to capturing carbon.
transcript.cn: 最好避免选择非林地景观，如天然草原、热带稀树草原或湿地，因为这些生态系统已经为捕获碳做出了巨大贡献。

transcript.en: It would also be advantageous to choose an area where trees could provide other benefits, such as recreational spaces.
transcript.cn: 选择一个树木可以提供其他好处的地方也是有利的，比如休闲场所。

transcript.en: Reforesting areas which are currently exploited for agriculture should be avoided as this often leads to other areas being deforested.
transcript.cn: 应避免在目前被用于农业的地区重新造林，因为这往往导致其他地区的森林被砍伐。

transcript.en: Large-scale reforestation projects require careful planning.
transcript.cn: 大规模的再造林项目需要周密的规划。

transcript.en: Making the right decisions about where to plant trees depends on having the right information.
transcript.cn: 要正确决定在哪里植树，必须掌握正确的信息。

transcript.en: Having detailed and up-to-date maps identifying high-priority areas for intervention is essential.
transcript.cn: 绘制详细的最新地图，确定优先干预区域至关重要。

transcript.en: Drone technology is a useful tool in helping to prioritise and monitor areas of degraded forest for restoration.
transcript.cn: 无人机技术非常有帮助，可以帮助确定恢复退化森林地区的优先次序并对其进行监测。

transcript.en: In Brazil, it's being used to identify and quantify how parts of the Amazon are being devastated by human activities such as rearing cattle and illegal logging.
transcript.cn: 在巴西，它被用来识别和量化亚马逊部分地区是如何被人类活动所破坏的，例如养牛和非法砍伐。

transcript.en: A good example of where the right trees were picked to achieve a restored forest is in Lampang Province in Northern Thailand.
transcript.cn: 泰国北部的南邦省就是一个很好的例子。

transcript.en: A previously forested site which had been degraded through mining was reforested by a cement company together with Chiang Mai University.
transcript.cn: 一家水泥公司与清迈大学合作，在一个因采矿而退化的森林地区重新植树造林。

transcript.en: After spreading 60 cm of topsoil, they planted 14 different native tree species which included several species of fig.
transcript.cn: 在铺上 60 厘米的表土后，他们种植了 14 种不同的本地树种，其中包括几种无花果。

transcript.en: Figs are a keystone species because of the critical role they play in maintaining wildlife populations.
transcript.cn: 无花果之所以是关键树种，是因为它们在维持野生动物数量方面发挥着至关重要的作用。

transcript.en: They are central to tropical reforestation projects as they accelerate the speed of the recovery process by attracting animals and birds which act as natural seed dispersers.
transcript.cn: 无花果是热带植树造林项目的核心，因为无花果能吸引动物和鸟类，成为天然的种子传播者，从而加快恢复进程。

transcript.en: This helps to promote diversity through the healthy regrowth of a wide range of plant species.
transcript.cn: 这有助于通过多种植物物种的健康再生来促进多样性。

transcript.en: Unlike the majority of fruit trees, figs bear fruit all year round, providing a reliable food source for many species.
transcript.cn: 与大多数果树不同，无花果全年结果，为许多物种提供了可靠的食物来源。

transcript.en: At this site, for example, after only three rainy seasons, monkeys started visiting to eat the fig fruits, naturally dispersing seeds through defecation.
transcript.cn: 例如，在这里，仅仅过了三个雨季，猴子就开始来吃无花果，并通过排便自然散播种子。

transcript.en: Reforestation projects should always aim to make sure that local communities are consulted and involved in the decision-making process.
transcript.cn: 植树造林项目应始终确保征求当地社区的意见，并让他们参与决策过程。

transcript.en: The restoration of mangrove forests in Madagascar is an example of a project which has succeeded in creating real benefits for the community.
transcript.cn: 马达加斯加红树林的恢复是一个成功地为社区创造实际利益的项目的例子。

transcript.en: Destruction of the mangrove forests had a terrible impact on plant and animal life, and also badly affected the fishing industry, which was a major source of employment for local people living in coastal areas.
transcript.cn: 红树林的破坏对动植物的生存造成了严重影响，也严重影响了渔业的发展，而渔业是沿海地区当地居民的主要就业来源。

transcript.en: The reforestation project involved hiring local people to plant and care for the new mangrove trees.
transcript.cn: 重新造林项目包括雇用当地人种植和养护新的红树林。

transcript.en: Millions of mangrove trees have now been planted which has resulted in the return of a healthy aquatic ecosystem.
transcript.cn: 现在，数百万棵红树林已经种植完毕，从而恢复了健康的水生生态系统。

transcript.en: The mangroves also act as a defence against the increased threat of flooding caused by climate change.
transcript.cn: 红树林还可以抵御气候变化造成的日益严重的洪水威胁。

transcript.en: What's more, the local economy is more stable and thousands more Madagascans are now able to send their children to school.
transcript.cn: 更重要的是，当地经济更加稳定，成千上万的马达加斯加人现在可以送孩子上学了。

transcript.en: One other important point to consider . . .
transcript.cn: 另一个需要考虑的重要问题是......
